title: Some users may experience errors or pages failing to load when accessing Microsoft 365 Copilot
impact: Users may have been unable to load pages when accessing Copilot.

updates.content:
Title: Some users may experience errors or pages failing to load when accessing Microsoft 365 Copilot

User impact: Users may experience errors or pages failing to load when accessing Copilot.

More info: Users may receive the following error message:

"We are sorry, something went wrong. Please try refreshing the page in a few minutes. If the problem persists, please visit status.cloud.microsoft for updates regarding known issues."

Some users may also encounter HTTP ERROR 502 responses, black screens, or sessions that remain stuck on a loading screen when accessing copilot.office.com or m365.cloud.microsoft.

Current status: We’ve received reports indicating an issue with accessing Copilot. We’re reviewing service monitoring telemetry to isolate the root cause and determine our next troubleshooting steps.

Scope of impact: Your organization is affected by this event, and some users attempting to access Copilot are impacted. This information may be updated as our investigation continues.

Next update by: Tuesday, April 7, 2026, at 11:00 AM UTC

Title: Some users may experience errors or pages failing to load when accessing Microsoft 365 Copilot

User impact: Users may experience errors or pages failing to load when accessing Copilot.

More info: Users may receive the following error message when attempting to access Copilot via m365.cloud.microsoft:

"We are sorry, something went wrong. Please try refreshing the page in a few minutes. If the problem persists, please visit status.cloud.microsoft for updates regarding known issues."

Some users may also encounter HTTP ERROR 502 responses, black screens, or sessions that remain stuck on a loading screen.

As a workaround, impacted users can continue to use Copilot in other applications, such as Microsoft Teams and the Outlook desktop client.

Current status: We’ve received reports indicating an issue with accessing Copilot. We’re reviewing service monitoring telemetry to isolate the root cause and determine our next troubleshooting steps.

Scope of impact: Impact is specific to some users in the UK, Central India, and Southeast Asia regions attempting to access Copilot via m365.cloud.microsoft. This information may be updated as our investigation continues.

Next update by: Tuesday, April 7, 2026, at 11:30 AM UTC

Title: Some users may experience errors or pages failing to load when accessing Microsoft 365 Copilot

User impact: Users may experience errors or pages failing to load when accessing Copilot.

More info: Users may receive the following error message when attempting to access Copilot via m365.cloud.microsoft:

"We are sorry, something went wrong. Please try refreshing the page in a few minutes. If the problem persists, please visit status.cloud.microsoft for updates regarding known issues."

Some users may also encounter HTTP ERROR 502 responses, black screens, or sessions that remain stuck on a loading screen.

A portion of users using the Copilot desktop app may also experience similar issues.

As a workaround, impacted users can continue to use Copilot in other applications, such as Microsoft Teams and the Outlook desktop client.

Current status: We’ve identified that a recent update to the Microsoft Copilot service increased user traffic in some regions beyond available capacity, resulting in impact. We’ve reverted the update to reduce load on the service and mitigate impact. We’re monitoring the service as traffic levels normalize and are continuing work to increase capacity in the affected regions to fully restore availability and help prevent recurrence.

Scope of impact: Impact is specific to some users in the UK, Central India, and Southeast Asia regions attempting to access Copilot either via m365.cloud.microsoft or via the Copilot desktop app. This information may be updated as our investigation continues.

Start time: Tuesday, April 7, 2026, at 6:15 AM UTC

Root cause: A recent update to the Microsoft Copilot service increased user traffic in some regions which is causing an imbalance in service traffic load, resulting in impact.

Next update by: Tuesday, April 7, 2026, at 1:00 PM UTC

Title: Some users may experience errors or pages failing to load when accessing Microsoft 365 Copilot

User impact: Users may experience errors or pages failing to load when accessing Copilot.

More info: Users may receive the following error message when attempting to access Copilot via m365.cloud.microsoft:

"We are sorry, something went wrong. Please try refreshing the page in a few minutes. If the problem persists, please visit status.cloud.microsoft for updates regarding known issues."

Some users may also encounter HTTP ERROR 502 responses, black screens, or sessions that remain stuck on a loading screen.

A portion of users using the Copilot desktop app may also experience similar issues.

As a workaround, impacted users can continue to use Copilot in other applications, such as Microsoft Teams and the Outlook desktop client.

Current status: We’ve completed reverting the aforementioned update and implementing additional routing changes to relieve traffic pressure in the affected regions. We’re seeing recovery across previously impacted areas and reaching out to a sample of affected users to confirm that the issue is resolved.

Scope of impact: Impact is specific to some users in the UK, Central India, and Southeast Asia regions attempting to access Copilot either via m365.cloud.microsoft or via the Copilot desktop app. This information may be updated as our investigation continues.

Start time: Tuesday, April 7, 2026, at 6:15 AM UTC

Root cause: A recent update to the Microsoft Copilot service increased user traffic in some regions which is causing an imbalance in service traffic load, resulting in impact.

Next update by: Tuesday, April 7, 2026, at 2:30 PM UTC

Title: Some users may experience errors or pages failing to load when accessing Microsoft 365 Copilot

User impact: Users may experience errors or pages failing to load when accessing Copilot.

More info: Users may receive the following error message when attempting to access Copilot via m365.cloud.microsoft:

"We are sorry, something went wrong. Please try refreshing the page in a few minutes. If the problem persists, please visit status.cloud.microsoft for updates regarding known issues."

Some users may also encounter HTTP ERROR 502 responses, black screens, or sessions that remain stuck on a loading screen.
A portion of users using the Copilot desktop app may also experience similar issues.

As a workaround, impacted users can continue to use Copilot in other applications, such as Microsoft Teams and the Outlook desktop client.

Current status: We’re awaiting confirmation from a sample of affected users that the issue is resolved.

Scope of impact: Impact is specific to some users in the UK, Central India, and Southeast Asia regions attempting to access Copilot either via m365.cloud.microsoft or via the Copilot desktop app. This information may be updated as our investigation continues.

Next update by: Tuesday, April 7, 2026, at 4:00 PM UTC

Title: Some users may have experienced errors or pages failing to load when accessing Microsoft 365 Copilot

User impact: Users may have been unable to load pages when accessing Copilot.

More info: Users may have received the following error message when attempting to access Copilot via m365.cloud.microsoft:
 
"We are sorry, something went wrong. Please try refreshing the page in a few minutes. If the problem persists, please visit status.cloud.microsoft for updates regarding known issues."
 
Some users may have also encountered HTTP ERROR 502 responses, black screens, or sessions that remained stuck on a loading screen. Some users on the Copilot desktop app may have also experienced similar issues.
 
As a workaround, impacted users could have continued to use Copilot in other applications, such as Microsoft Teams and the Outlook desktop client.

Final status: We've confirmed through our service health telemetry that reversing our most recent update remediated the impact.

Scope of impact: Impact was specific to some users in the United Kingdom, Central India, and Southeast Asia regions attempting to access Copilot either via m365.cloud.microsoft or via the Copilot desktop app.

Start time: Tuesday, April 7, 2026, at 6:15 AM UTC

End time: Tuesday, April 7, 2026, at 11:51 AM UTC

Root cause: A recent update to the Microsoft Copilot service increased user traffic in some regions that caused an imbalance in service traffic load, resulting in impact.

Next steps: 
 - We're reviewing our service update testing and validation procedures to identify and prevent similar issues with load balancing in deployments moving forward.

This is the final update for the event.
